Warm sanctuary at dusk, ancient war council, safe but haunted. Loopable instrumental Eclipse boss battle music for a dark medieval fantasy tactical game, three and a half minutes long. Start with immediate tension and percussion, no slow empty intro. 30-second one-shot cinematic intro score for a dark medieval fantasy tactical game, instrumental, no vocals, not loopable. The visual sequence starts almost black with fog and an ominous eclipse, then reveals broken roads, distant crows, a fortress under violet light, lightning strikes, a shadow boss silhouette, and finally a golden title crest. Timing and structure: 0-3s near-silent ominous low drone, distant wind, very soft low strings, faint thunder flash around 2.2s. 3-7s eclipse reveal, slow dark cello and contrabass swell, subtle medieval frame drum heartbeat, distant crows implied by sparse high string scratches, mysterious but restrained. 7-11s camera moves across broken roads, add cautious rhythmic pulse, low hand drums and muted violin ostinato, lightning accent around 9.5s. 11-15s fortress focus, deeper brass and choir texture without words, ancient stone resonance, controlled tension building. 15-19s boss shadow reveal, two sharp lightning-orchestral hits around 15.4s and 16.4s, short camera-shake impact feel, dark brass, low drums, threatening supernatural energy. 19-22s release into heroic but grim call-to-arms, tension relaxes slightly, strings rise with restrained epic warmth. 22-24s title crest reveal, golden but melancholic resolution, noble brass chord and soft bells. 24-30s very low sustained tail for the enter button, not silent, no new melody, gentle fade-ready ending. Style: dark medieval fantasy, mysterious, melancholic, tense, cinematic, subtle epic finale. Instruments: low strings, contrabass, cello, muted violins, frame drums, low war drums, dark brass, distant bells, restrained wordless choir texture. No modern electronic trailer sound, no happy tone, no pop rhythm, no lyrics, no copyrighted style references.
